ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

PTR-18-302408 & PTR-20-303831

Appearances: Andrew Weil, via ZOOM, counsel for Simon Weinberg and Sebastian Weinberg, Petitioners; John Rueppel, via ZOOM, counsel for Terry Weinberg, Trustee.

Counsel presented oral argument regarding the several motions and the Order to Show Cause issued by the court on April 26, 2021. The court finds that sanctions are appropriate. Stay of Discovery is lifted and Discovery Motion Hearings are continued - see order for continuance dates. Terry Weinberg's Ex Parte Application is Denied.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR#: 10619, email: raquel@transcriptslegal.com, phone number: 925.384.0003

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Continued To Jun-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-18-302408 The Suzanne Bette Weinberg 2018 Revocable Trust and PTR-20-303831 The Murray Weinberg and S. Bette Weinberg 1984 Trust.

Appearances: Marc Mazer and Andrew Weill appeared by zoom for Simon Weinberg and Sebastian Weinberg.
Evan Winet and John Rueppel appeared by zoom for and with Terry Weinberg by zoom.

Mr. Rueppel stated that Dana filed an opposition, that Dana passed away, that the Personal Representatives never appeared in this matter and raised the issue of the petition needing to be amended to add Dana's estate as a beneficiary. The Court stated that they need to get a temporary trustee in place so that the trusts can move forward, that it does not intend to remove Mr. Weinberg as the trustee as it requires an evidentiary hearing, but Mr. Weinberg will remain suspended and that it will appoint a temporary trustee. Mr. Rueppel stated that he believes he has complied with the Court's order and spoke of Mr. Weinberg's fiduciary duty to protect the assets even after he is suspended and until those assets are out of his hands. The Court stated that Mr. Weinberg is not entitled to represent himself as the trustee without indicating that he is suspended, that the two trusts have the same corpus and questions how it can go forward. Mr. Rueppel stated that it's been that way for several years with no indecent, that the trusts are not the same corpus and that Mr. Weinberg will continue to manage the trust to find out what assets are in it. Mr. Weill stated that they are the same assets. The Court noted that it only has a list of three fiduciaries who have agreed to serve. Discussion was held with respect to Mr. Sugarman's legal background and the temporary trustee not being represented by Mr. Weill's firm. The Court will appoint David A. Bradlow as the temporary trustee with independent representation of Counsel and with bond set at $300,000.00. Mr. Weill is to file a consent to serve from Mr. Bradlow and prepare a form of order. The Petition to Confirm Ownership of Real Property, etc., the Petition for Financial Elder Abuse, etc., the Petition to Appoint Simon Weinberg as Successor Trustee, etc. and the orders to show cause (2) are continued to June 8, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to be talking to get the petitions in shape for an evidentiary hearing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition Of Simon And Sebastian Weinberg To Appoint Simon Weinberg As Trustee Of Murray Weinberg And S. Bette Weinberg 1984 Trust Due To Death Of Prior Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-19-2021 Denied

Petition Of Simon And Sebastian Weinberg To Appoint Simon Weinberg As Trustee Of Murray Weinberg And S. Bette Weinberg 1984 Trust Due To Death Of Prior Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-16-2021 Continued To Mar-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-18-302408 The Suzanne Bette Weinberg 2018 Revocable Trust and PTR-20-303831 The Murray Weinberg and S. Bette Weinberg 1984 Trust.

Appearances: Mark Mazer and Andrew Weill appeared by zoom for Simon Weinberg (Respondent) and Sebastian Weinberg (Respondent).
Evan Winet appeared by zoom for Terry Weinberg (Trustee).

The Court stated that it received the status statement from Mr. Rueppel and Mr. Winet, that it excuses the non-appearance of Mr. Rueppel without issuing sanctions and that the most pressing matter is who will manage these two trusts. The Court also stated that the two trusts are the same corpus, that it has received numerous allegations of misconduct, that the only viable solution is to deny Simon's petition to be confirmed as the Successor Trustee of the Murray and Bette Weinberg Trust and suspend Terry as the Trustee of the Murray Weinberg Trust, and to appoint a neutral trustee on both trusts. The parties expressed their concerns regarding the additional costs of having a neutral trustee, spoke of various stipulations (property management/maintaining status quo), setting a trial date. discovery etc. The Court informed the parties that it will be issuing an order to show cause regarding the issue of removal and the issue of appointment of a neutral successor trustee for March 30, 2021. The parties are to show why Terry should not be removed. The parties are to meet and confer regarding a neutral professional fiduciary for both trusts. If the parties agree on someone, they are to speak with the professional fiduciary to see if they are willing to accept the appointment. If the parties cannot agree, the parties are to submit up to three professional fiduciaries that have been talked to and are willing to serve. The parties are also to meet and confer regarding the amount of bond for the successor trustee. If they are unable to agree on an amount of bond, they are to submit their positions. In addition, the Court informed Counsel that it will eventually issue a case management order that sets a trial schedule. The Petition to Confirm Ownership of Real Property, etc., the Petition for Financial Elder Abuse, etc., and the Petition to Appoint Simon Weinberg as Successor Trustee, etc. are continued to March 30, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Reporter: Heidi Ryder, CSR #10053, 707-480-1408, hjrydercsr@outlook.com

Petition Of Simon And Sebastian Weinberg To Appoint Simon Weinberg As Trustee Of Murray Weinberg And S. Bette Weinberg 1984 Trust Due To Death Of Prior Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Continued To Feb-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-18-302408 The Suzanne Bette Weinberg 2018 Revocable Trust and PTR-20-303831 The Murray Weinberg and S. Bette Weinberg 1984 Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Weill and Marc Mazer appeared by zoom for Simon Weinberg and Sebastian Weinberg (Respondents).

The Court noted that no one was present on behalf of Terry Weinberg. Mr. Weill stated that he is not aware as to why no one is present and that they had e-mail communication last week. The Court passed the matter to the end of the calendar. The Court Clerk contacted Mr. Rueppel's office and communicated with the Receptionist who attempted to contact John Rueppel, Evan Winet and Counsel's Assistant, but was unable to get through to anyone. The Court noted that the matters were properly noticed, that there are two different trusts with different beneficiaries and the same corpus, and asked how this is working. Mr. Weill stated that it was working up until the death of Mr. Weinberg and that the properties are under the management of an agreed Property Manager. Mr. Mazer stated that Terry opposed the petition to appoint Simon because Terry thinks it is unnecessary to have anyone as Trustee in the 1984 Trust, not because of who Simon is. The Court stated that it is quite displeased for Terry not showing up, that it spent much time preparing for this matter and that it will not act today. The issue of sanctions against Mr. Rueppel was mentioned. The Petition to Confirm Ownership of Real Property, etc., the Petition for Financial Elder Abuse, etc., and the Petition to Appoint Simon Weinberg as Successor Trustee, etc. are continued to February 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the trustee issue. If the parties are unable to agree to Simon being the Successor Trustee, there needs to be an alternate neutral third-party fiduciary for the entire thing. Counsel are to communicate this to Mr. Rueppel.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
